task_completion.visual_evidence: The UGENE Workflow Designer graphical interface should be visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Add and configure an input block to read the specified FASTA file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Read Sequence' (or similar) input block on the workflow canvas, with its properties/parameters showing the file path '~/UGENE_Data/hemoglobin_beta_multispecies.fasta'.

task_completion.description: Add a ClustalW alignment block to the workflow
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'ClustalW' block placed on the workflow canvas and visually connected with a line from the input block.

task_completion.description: Add and configure an output block to write the alignment results
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Write Alignment' (or similar) output block connected to the ClustalW block, with its properties showing the output path '~/UGENE_Data/workflow_results/hemoglobin_aligned.aln'.

task_completion.description: Save the completed workflow schema as a .uwl file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Evidence of the workflow being saved as 'alignment_pipeline.uwl' in the 'workflow_results' directory, such as the filename appearing in the UGENE window title, a 'Save As' dialog, or a file manager/terminal showing the created file.

integrity.description: The agent used the UGENE Workflow Designer GUI to build the pipeline
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the UGENE graphical interface, rather than the agent writing XML/UWL code in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent constructed a visual workflow rather than running command-line tools
integrity.visual_evidence: The workflow canvas should display connected graphical blocks for reading, aligning, and writing data.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to fake the output files
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal commands like 'touch alignment_pipeline.uwl' or 'echo > hemoglobin_aligned.aln' used to bypass the actual workflow creation and execution.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file is located at /home/ga/UGENE_Data/hemoglobin_beta_multispecies.fasta and contains exactly 8 sequences. The expected workflow schema output must be saved to /home/ga/UGENE_Data/workflow_results/alignment_pipeline.uwl. The expected alignment output must be configured to write to /home/ga/UGENE_Data/workflow_results/hemoglobin_aligned.aln.
